segments.text: But Job answered and said,
Oh that my grief were throughly weighed, and my calamity laid in the balances together!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For now it would be heavier than the sand of the sea: therefore my words are swallowed up.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For the arrows of the Almighty are within me, the poison whereof drinketh up my spirit: the terrors of God do set themselves in array against me.
segments.speaker: Job

footnotes: my words…: that is, I want words to express my grief

segments.text: Doth the wild ass bray when he hath grass? or loweth the ox over his fodder?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Can that which is unsavoury be eaten without salt? or is there any taste in the white of an egg?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The things that my soul refused to touch are as my sorrowful meat.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Oh that I might have my request; and that God would grant me the thing that I long for!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Even that it would please God to destroy me; that he would let loose his hand, and cut me off!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Then should I yet have comfort; yea, I would harden myself in sorrow: let him not spare; for I have not concealed the words of the Holy One.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: What is my strength, that I should hope? and what is mine end, that I should prolong my life?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Is my strength the strength of stones? or is my flesh of brass?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Is not my help in me? and is wisdom driven quite from me?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: To him that is afflicted pity should be shewed from his friend; but he forsaketh the fear of the Almighty.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My brethren have dealt deceitfully as a brook, and as the stream of brooks they pass away;
Which are blackish by reason of the ice, and wherein the snow is hid:
What time they wax warm, they vanish: when it is hot, they are consumed out of their place.

footnotes: when…: Heb. in the heat thereof

segments.text: The paths of their way are turned aside; they go to nothing, and perish.
The troops of Tema looked, the companies of Sheba waited for them.
They were confounded because they had hoped; they came thither, and were ashamed.
For now ye are nothing; ye see my casting down, and are afraid.
segments.speaker: Job

footnotes: ye are…: or, ye are like to them: Heb. to it

segments.text: Did I say, Bring unto me? or, Give a reward for me of your substance?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Or, Deliver me from the enemy's hand? or, Redeem me from the hand of the mighty?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Teach me, and I will hold my tongue: and cause me to understand wherein I have erred.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: How forcible are right words! but what doth your arguing reprove?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Do ye imagine to reprove words, and the speeches of one that is desperate, which are as wind?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Yea, ye overwhelm the fatherless, and ye dig a pit for your friend.
segments.speaker: Job

footnotes: ye overwhelm: Heb. ye cause to fall upon

segments.text: Now therefore be content, look upon me; for it is evident unto you if I lie.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Return, I pray you, let it not be iniquity; yea, return again, my righteousness is in it.
segments.speaker: Job

footnotes: in it: that is, in this matter

segments.text: Is there iniquity in my tongue? cannot my taste discern perverse things?
segments.speaker: Job
